2606.25519.title: Quantization Inflates Reasoning: Token Inflation as a Hidden Cost of Low-Bit Reasoning Models
2606.25519.paper_summary: Quantization is widely used to reduce the inference cost of large language models, but its effect on reasoning models is not fully captured by final-answer accuracy or per-token latency. We show that low-bit post-training quantization can introduce a hidden test-time compute cost: quantized reasoning models often generate longer chains of thought even when they still answer correctly. Across mathematical reasoning, code generation, scientific question answering, and agentic tool-use benchmarks, we find that INT4/INT3 quantization can preserve accuracy but increase reasoning-token usage, offsetting the expected per-token speedup. To measure this effect, we introduce the CoT Token Inflation Ratio, which compares reasoning length between quantized and full-precision models averaged across all evaluation benchmarks. We further show that token inflation is accompanied by behavioral changes in the reasoning trace, including more intermediate steps and greater semantic repetition. These changes translate into measurable end-to-end real-world serving penalties. Finally, we evaluate mitigation strategies and find that prompting and decoding-time sampling offer inconsistent accuracy-length trade-offs, while quantization-aware training shows more promise in reducing both accuracy degradation and token inflation. Our results suggest that reasoning-token usage should be reported alongside accuracy when evaluating quantized reasoning models.
2606.25519.paper_summary_zh: 量化被廣泛用於降低大型語言模型的推理成本，但其對推理模型的影響並未完全透過最終答案的準確性或每個標記的延遲來捕捉。我們展示了低位後訓練量化可能引入隱藏的測試時間計算成本：量化的推理模型即使在正確回答的情況下，通常也會生成更長的思考鏈。在數學推理、代碼生成、科學問答和代理工具使用基準中，我們發現 INT4/INT3 量化可以保持準確性，但會增加推理標記的使用，抵消預期的每標記加速。為了測量這一效果，我們引入了 CoT 標記通脹比率，該比率比較量化模型與全精度模型在所有評估基準上的平均推理長度。我們進一步顯示，標記通脹伴隨著推理過程中的行為變化，包括更多的中間步驟和更大的語義重複。這些變化轉化為可測量的端到端現實世界服務懲罰。最後，我們評估了緩解策略，發現提示和解碼時間取樣提供不一致的準確性與長度的權衡，而量化感知訓練在減少準確性下降和標記通脹方面顯示出更多的潛力。我們的結果表明，在評估量化推理模型時，應該將推理標記的使用與準確性一起報告。
2606.25519.author: Xinyu Lian et.al.
2606.25519.authors: Xinyu Lian, Walid Krichene, Beichen Huang, Masahiro Tanaka, Olatunji Ruwase, Li Zhang, Minjia Zhang
